purpose: Pre-PR verification policy for coding agents changing AccessiView.
deterministicBeforePrRules: Run the blocking verification command after code changes and before publishing a PR.
Do not publish a PR while static checks, manifest checks, fixture smoke tests, privacy checks, color readability checks, or automation coverage assertions fail.
Fix failing deterministic checks, rerun them, and report the final command result.
Add or update a local fixture when fixing a live-site or layout-specific regression.
Keep browser extension permissions and content security policy unchanged unless the feature explicitly requires reviewed changes.
State any skipped relevant check and the reason before publishing.

runWhen: A change affects main-content detection, simplification, focus reader, motion handling, forms, summaries, popup active-tab routing, custom site rules, or built-in site rules.
A deterministic fixture was added because of a live-site regression.
A PR is large enough that one fixture class cannot represent the affected behavior.
safetyRules: Use public pages that do not require login.
Do not submit forms, create accounts, purchase items, post content, or change user data.
Do not collect personal data, cookies, session tokens, page text dumps, or private screenshots.
Record consent prompts, paywalls, CAPTCHA, anti-automation blocks, and regional redirects as site conditions instead of deterministic failures.

failurePolicy: Blocking command failure: fix before publishing.
Reproducible live-site extension regression: create or update a local fixture, fix the regression, and rerun blocking checks.
Live-site-only instability caused by external site behavior: report as advisory and do not block the PR by itself.
Unrun relevant check: state why it was skipped and what risk remains.
